title: Citharexylum brachyanthum
intro: Citharexylum brachyanthum, also known as the "Wild Fuchsia" or "Tree Fuchsia", is a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae, native to Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Citharexylum brachyanthum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Citharexylum brachyanthum is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into herbal blends and supplements to support immune function and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Citharexylum brachyanthum is classified within the family Verbenaceae. It belongs to the genus Citharexylum, which consists of various species of flowering plants. The species Citharexylum brachyanthum is native to the Americas and is commonly found in tropical regions. Within the Verbenaceae family, Citharexylum brachyanthum is closely related to other species such as Citharexylum punctatum and Lantana camara. Further classification of Citharexylum brachyanthum is based on its botanical characteristics, including leaf shape and flower color.
